what_it_does: An acceptance test script for the SUITE-3 wiring lane. It verifies that live callers actually invoke the newly built cognition substrate: Suite.cognition_info passes the six corpus registries and projections, the MCP capture tool calls embed_corpus_to_spaces to populate queryable vector spaces with lineage written before embedding, the run_role wrapper persists finish_reason and usage through a meta out-param into op.run records, and route_run_output/saved-cascade helpers exist and honor the floor that computation/telemetry sites must not emit resolve/approve/dispatch actions.
observations: Uses monkeypatching on mcp_face.server and runtime.cognition to stub the model, embedder, and role engine for hermetic tests.
Defines a PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that prints ok/XX and raises SystemExit on failure.

Section [1] asserts cognition_info returns non-empty lists for projections, lifters, mark_types, generation_policies, relation_types, ai_tics, and forms, and uses inspect.getsource to ensure no redundant in-lane augment remains.

Section [2] stubs per-space distinct vector geometry, intercepts embed_corpus_to_spaces to inject the stub, verifies capture writes lineage before embedding, lands vectors at space_address, and runs find_relations end-to-end over the capture-written vectors.
Section [3] stubs run_role to fill meta.finish_reason and meta.usage, then checks the return value, the op.run event, and find_runs; also tests the honest-absent case when meta is not filled.
Section [4] checks existence of Suite.route_run_output, Suite.save_cascade, and Suite.run_cascade, routes a run output to an address, and confirms DESTINATION_KINDS excludes resolve/approve/dispatch.
Section [5] scans the source bodies of capture, capture_corpus, and cognition_info for forbidden action calls (resolve_surfaced, dispatch_decision, .approve(, drive_dispatchable).
The file repeatedly notes that a live :8001 embedder is out of scope and marked as needs-tim.
standouts: Hermetic proof of the full capture -> embed_corpus_to_spaces -> space_address vector write -> find_relations pipeline using a stub embedder.
Source-code scanning for forbidden resolve/approve/dispatch symbols as a concrete floor enforcement mechanism.
Meta out-param test that proves finish_reason and usage are threaded from the engine through the MCP wrapper into the op.run record and find_runs results.
conventions: Inline acceptance-test script organized into numbered sections with descriptive headers and a simple check-based assertion style.
Monkeypatches server globals and engine functions to avoid external model/vector services during wiring tests.
Uses inspect.getsource string matching to verify code-level removal of redundant mechanisms and to enforce the no-action floor.
Creates a fresh temporary FsStore, NodeRegistry, and Suite for each test section to isolate state.
concerns: Heavy monkeypatching of internal module bindings (srv.SUITE, srv._cog.run_items, srv._resolve_role, srv._cog.embed_corpus_to_spaces, srv._cog.run_role) makes the test brittle to internal refactorings.
Relies on inspect.getsource string matching for correctness, which can break with formatting, docstring, or import alias changes.
Imports mcp_face.server inside test sections, so side effects and import-time initialization are not visible in this file alone.
notes: A wiring acceptance test that uses stubs and source scanning to prove the cognition substrate is live at its caller sites while staying below the resolve/approve/dispatch floor.
questions: What are the exact schemas and discovery rules for the six corpus registries populated from the nodes directory?
How does the production embedder at :8001 handle degraded mode, and how is that surfaced when capture runs without the stub?
What is the exact contract between Suite.capture_corpus and the MCP capture tool, and which face is responsible for lineage assembly?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it proves the specified call-sites are wired and live, plus asserts the negative floor against action emission.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud floor against action emission from computation sites
intention_signals: The file is trying to demonstrate that the newly built cognition substrate is not merely present but actually invoked by its intended callers, and that those callers remain purely computational/telemetry-oriented.
novelty: Combines runtime stubbing with static source-body scanning in one acceptance file to enforce both positive wiring assertions and a negative no-action floor.
contribution: A wiring acceptance test that documents and guards the hand-off points between the cognition substrate and its callers, including positive embed-on-write, finish_reason persistence, and a negative floor against emitting resolve/approve/dispatch decisions.
summary_for_embedding: SUITE-3 wiring acceptance test that stubs external services to prove cognition_info registry passing, capture-driven embed-on-write, run_role finish_reason persistence, route_run_output/cascade existence, and a no-action floor.
intention_for_embedding: To verify that the cognition substrate's live callers are wired correctly and remain non-action-producing computation/telemetry paths.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that prints ok/XX and raises SystemExit on failure.
symbols.description: Implements the test harness assertion primitive. It prints a status line for each label, increments a global PASS counter when the condition is true, and aborts the whole run with a FAIL message when the condition is false.

symbols.does: Factory that builds a hermetic Suite over a temporary FsStore and discovered nodes.
symbols.description: Discovers nodes under ROOT/nodes via NodeRegistry and returns a Suite backed by an FsStore at the supplied root, used to isolate every test section.

symbols.does: Returns a stub embed_fn that maps input texts to per-space vectors.
symbols.description: Creates a closure over a lookup table and a default vector so the test can assign deterministic, per-space geometry to captured or seeded texts without calling a live embedding service.

symbols.does: Inner stub embedder returning one vector per input text.
symbols.description: The actual function passed as embed_fn; it looks up each input string in the table and falls back to the default vector, matching the (transport, inputs, model, dim) seam expected by the embedding path.

symbols.does: Simple stand-in for a run_items result object.
symbols.description: A minimal class with resolved, skipped, failed, and wall_s attributes used to stub the model fan-out inside the capture wiring test so the post-run write-and-embed path can be exercised deterministically.

symbols.does: Intercepts embed_corpus_to_spaces to capture records and inject a stub embedder.
symbols.description: Wraps the real embed_corpus_to_spaces function, records the records handed to it, then supplies a stub embed_fn, dimension, and model before delegating, letting the test prove capture assembles the correct record shape and that the real embedding code path runs.

symbols.does: Stub engine run_role that fills finish_reason and usage into the meta dict.
symbols.description: Simulates the engine side of the O3 out-param seam: it asserts meta is a dict, writes finish_reason and usage into it, and returns a validated-shape result so the wrapper's persistence can be verified.

symbols.does: Stub engine run_role that does not populate meta.
symbols.description: Used to test the honest-absent case: when the engine does not fill meta, the wrapper must report finish_reason as None rather than fabricating a value.
